title: Earnings call transcript: SNDL Inc reports Q2 2025 earnings beat with positive EPS surprise
text: Sundial Growers (SNDL) delivered an impressive earnings performance for the second quarter of 2025, exceeding analyst expectations on both profitability and revenue metrics. The company reported earnings per share of $0.01, dramatically outperforming the forecasted loss of $0.05. Revenue climbed to $244.8 million, surpassing the anticipated $231.55 million figure. This represented 7.3% year-over-year growth, notably outpacing the broader Canadian recreational cannabis market expansion of approximately 3-4%. A historic milestone emerged as SNDL achieved positive operating income for the first time in company history, reaching $5.8 million (adjusted). CEO Zach George emphasized the achievement, stating: 2025 marks a pivotal milestone for SNDL as we report positive operating income and net earnings for the first time. The stock surged 14.58% in pre-market trading following the announcement. Key growth drivers included the cannabis retail segment's 11% revenue increase, the acquisition of One Centimeters to expand retail presence, and the Rise Rewards loyalty program, which already accumulated six-figure membership numbers. International expansion contributed $3.8 million in sales, with management expecting meaningful growth in subsequent quarters from UK and European markets.
